question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty channel option for SO_REUSEADDR in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause a network partition scenario where a server restart after a partition fails because the port is still in TIME_WAIT, preventing the server from recovering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty channel option for IP_TOS, causing the OS to deprioritize gRPC traffic during a network congestion event that resembles a partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty HTTP/2 GOAWAY handling configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause a network partition scenario where the server sends GOAWAY but the client never receives it due to the partition, causing the client to keep sending requests that are silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty channel option for SO_KEEPALIVE with a short interval, so that during a partition the OS-level keepalive (default 2 hours) never fires and zombie connections accumulate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty HTTP/2 initial window size configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause a network partition scenario where the default large window size allows the server to buffer large amounts of data for a partitioned client, exhausting heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty channel option for TCP_KEEPIDLE, so that the OS waits the default idle time (7200 seconds) before sending keepalive probes, allowing partitioned connections to persist for hours?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty HTTP/2 max concurrent streams configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() beyond the per-connection maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection, to cause a network partition scenario where the server's HTTP/2 layer and the application layer have inconsistent stream limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty channel option for TCP_KEEPINTVL, so that even when keepalive probes are sent, the interval between probes is the OS default (75 seconds), allowing partitioned connections to persist for minutes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a Netty HTTP/2 max frame size configuration in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause a network partition scenario where the server accepts very large DATA frames that fill the Netty write buffer during a partition, causing memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition outside design parameters by exploiting the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a Netty channel option for TCP_KEEPCNT, so that the OS sends the default number of keepalive probes (9) before closing the connection, allowing partitioned connections to persist for over 11 minutes?
